Expected_Result: Serves static SPA — 200

Steps: Send GET to /swagger.json

Steps: Send POST /api/chat with Origin: https://evil.com header
Expected_Result: Only allowed origins should receive CORS response headers
Note: Wildcard CORS allows any origin — should restrict to known domains

Steps: Send POST /api/chat with Origin: http://localhost:3000 header
Expected_Result: Only allowed origins should receive CORS response headers
Note: Local frontend origin — expected to be allowed

Steps: Send POST /api/chat with Origin: null header
Expected_Result: Only allowed origins should receive CORS response headers
Note: Null origin allowed by wildcard CORS — file:// attack vector

Steps: Send POST /api/chat with Origin: https://attacker.io header
Expected_Result: Only allowed origins should receive CORS response headers
Note: Arbitrary origin allowed due to allow_origins=['*']

Steps: Send POST /api/chat with Origin: http://internal.corp header
Expected_Result: Only allowed origins should receive CORS response headers
Note: Internal origin also allowed — no allowlist enforced

Steps: Send POST /api/chat with Origin: https://sub.evil.com header
Expected_Result: Only allowed origins should receive CORS response headers
Note: Subdomain of malicious domain allowed

Steps: Send POST /api/chat with Origin: MISSING header
Expected_Result: Only allowed origins should receive CORS response headers
Note: Requests without Origin header should still work

Steps: Send POST /api/chat with Origin: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net header
Expected_Result: Only allowed origins should receive CORS response headers
Note: CDN origin allowed — may enable cross-origin data access

Steps: Send POST /api/chat with Origin: https://localhost.evil.com header
Expected_Result: Only allowed origins should receive CORS response headers
Note: Localhost-looking malicious domain allowed by wildcard CORS

Steps: Send POST /api/chat with Origin: https://xss.evil.com/payload header
Expected_Result: Only allowed origins should receive CORS response headers
Note: CORS wildcard combined with credentials allows cross-site attacks

Steps: Send POST with payload and auth: No Authorization header
Expected_Result: Server should enforce authentication for sensitive AI endpoints
Note: Endpoint is public — no auth enforced. This is the root design issue.

Steps: Send POST with payload and auth: Invalid Bearer token
Expected_Result: Server should enforce authentication for sensitive AI endpoints
Note: Server ignores auth headers — no JWT middleware

Steps: Send POST with payload and auth: Expired JWT token
Expected_Result: Server should enforce authentication for sensitive AI endpoints
Note: Expired JWTs should be rejected if auth is enforced

Steps: Send POST with payload and auth: Malformed JWT (2 parts)
Expected_Result: Server should enforce authentication for sensitive AI endpoints
Note: Malformed JWT — no verification layer

Steps: Send POST with payload and auth: JWT with 'none' algorithm
Expected_Result: Server should enforce authentication for sensitive AI endpoints
Note: Algorithm 'none' attack — server accepts unsigned JWTs

Steps: Send POST with payload and auth: SQL in Authorization
Expected_Result: Server should enforce authentication for sensitive AI endpoints
Note: Injection in auth header

Steps: Send POST with payload and auth: Very long auth header (10KB)
Expected_Result: Server should enforce authentication for sensitive AI endpoints
Note: Header size overflow probe

Steps: Send POST with payload and auth: Base64-encoded fake token
Expected_Result: Server should enforce authentication for sensitive AI endpoints
Note: Basic auth on JWT-style endpoint

Steps: Send POST with payload and auth: Empty Bearer value
Expected_Result: Server should enforce authentication for sensitive AI endpoints
Note: Empty Bearer value — edge case

Steps: Send POST with payload and auth: Multiple Authorization headers
Expected_Result: Server should enforce authentication for sensitive AI endpoints
Note: Multiple auth header values

Steps: Transport security test: HTTP downgrade — plain HTTP access
Expected_Result: All communications must be over TLS 1.2+ with valid certificates
Note: Server listens on plain HTTP — no TLS enforcement, data in transit unencrypted

Steps: Transport security test: No HTTPS redirect from HTTP
Expected_Result: All communications must be over TLS 1.2+ with valid certificates
Note: No 301 redirect to HTTPS — users may stay on insecure HTTP

Steps: Transport security test: TLSv1.0 supported (if HTTPS added)
Expected_Result: All communications must be over TLS 1.2+ with valid certificates
Note: Legacy TLS 1.0 should be disabled — vulnerable to POODLE, BEAST

Steps: Transport security test: TLSv1.1 supported (if HTTPS added)
Expected_Result: All communications must be over TLS 1.2+ with valid certificates
Note: Legacy TLS 1.1 should be disabled

Steps: Transport security test: TLSv1.2 with weak ciphers
Expected_Result: All communications must be over TLS 1.2+ with valid certificates
Note: TLS 1.2 with RC4/3DES — weak cipher suites

Steps: Transport security test: TLSv1.3 required for production
Expected_Result: All communications must be over TLS 1.2+ with valid certificates
Note: TLS 1.3 is the secure minimum — verify when HTTPS is deployed

Steps: Transport security test: Certificate chain validation
Expected_Result: All communications must be over TLS 1.2+ with valid certificates
Note: Ensure valid CA-signed certificate, not self-signed in production

Steps: Transport security test: HSTS preload not configured
Expected_Result: All communications must be over TLS 1.2+ with valid certificates
Note: HSTS preload not in HSTS header — browsers may not cache HTTPS requirement

Scenario: Mixed content warnings (HTTP resources on HTTPS page)
Steps: Transport security test: Mixed content warnings (HTTP resources on HTTPS page)
Expected_Result: All communications must be over TLS 1.2+ with valid certificates
Note: Static assets served over HTTP if HTTPS not enforced
